revision.text:
[[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|Crest of the Katakura clan]] The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from [[Katō Kagekiyo]]. The family entered [[Mutsu Province]] in the 1300s as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the [[Date clan]], and remained so until 1872. The heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''[[karō]]'' in the [[Sendai Domain]].
==References== *{{ja icon}} [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/katakura.html "Katakura-shi" on Harimaya.com] (9 March 2008)
{{samurai-stub}}
[[Category:Japanese clans]]
[[ja:片倉氏]]

[[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|Crest of the Katakura clan]] The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered [[Mutsu Province]] in the 1300s as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the [[Date clan]], and remained so until 1872. The heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''[[karō]]'' in the [[Sendai Domain]].
==References== *{{ja icon}} [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/katakura.html "Katakura-shi" on Harimaya.com] (9 March 2008)
{{samurai-stub}}
[[Category:Japanese clans]]
[[ja:片倉氏]]

[[Shigenobu Katakura]], the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is the direct descendant of this family.

[[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|Crest of the Katakura clan]] The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered [[Mutsu Province]] in the 1300s as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the [[Date clan]], and remained so until 1872. In the [[Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, [[Katakura Kagetsuna]], became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to [[Date Masamune]]).
In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''[[karō]]'' in the [[Sendai Domain]].
[[Shigenobu Katakura]], the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is the direct descendant of this family.
==References== *{{ja icon}} [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/katakura.html "Katakura-shi" on Harimaya.com] (9 March 2008) *{{ja icon}} [http://www008.upp.so-net.ne.jp/tomeas/katakura.htm Family tree of the Katakura clan]
{{samurai-stub}} {{surname-stub}}
[[Category:Japanese clans]]
[[ja:片倉氏]]

The family's head, [[Katakura Kagetsuna]], became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to [[Date Masamune]]).
In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''[[karō]]'' in the [[Sendai Domain]]. Their personal fief was centered at [[Shiroishi Castle]].

[[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|140px|The emblem ([[Mon (badge)|''mon'']]) of the Katakura clan]] {{Japanese name|Katakura}} The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from [[Fujiwara no Toshihito]] by way of [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered [[Mutsu Province]] in the 14th century as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the [[Date clan]], and remained so until 1872. In the [[Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, [[Katakura Kagetsuna]], became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to [[Date Masamune]]).
In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''[[karō]]'' in the [[Sendai Domain]]. Their personal fief was centered at [[Shiroishi Castle]] (modern-day [[Shiroishi, Miyagi]]).
[[Shigenobu Katakura]], the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family. ==Head Family== # Katakura Kagekatsu # Katakura Kagefusa # Katakura Kagenobu # Katakura Kageharu # Katakura Kagetsune # Katakura Kagetoki # Katakura Kageshige # Katakura Kagesuke # Katakura Kageyuki # Katakura Kagehiro # Katakura Kagemura # Katakura Kageshige # [[Katakura Kagetsuna]] # Katakura Kage # Katakura Kage
==References== *{{ja icon}} [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/katakura.html "Katakura-shi" on Harimaya.com] (9 March 2008) *{{ja icon}} [http://www008.upp.so-net.ne.jp/tomeas/katakura.htm Family tree of the Katakura clan] (9 March 2008)
{{DEFAULTSORT:Katakura clan}} [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Katakura clan]]
{{Japan-clan-stub}}
[[ja:片倉氏]]

[[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|140px|The emblem ([[Mon (badge)|''mon'']]) of the Katakura clan]] {{Japanese name|Katakura}} The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from [[Fujiwara no Toshihito]] by way of [[Katō Kagekado]]. ==References== *{{ja icon}} [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/katakura.html "Katakura-shi" on Harimaya.com] (9 March 2008) *{{ja icon}} [http://www008.upp.so-net.ne.jp/tomeas/katakura.htm Family tree of the Katakura clan] (9 March 2008)

revision.text:
{{Use dmy dates|date=March 2020}} {{family name hatnote|Katakura|lang=Japanese}} [[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|140px|The emblem ([[Mon (badge)|''mon'']]) of the Katakura clan]] The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from [[Fujiwara no Toshihito]] by way of [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered [[Mutsu Province]] in the 14th century as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the [[Date clan]], and remained so until 1872. In the [[Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, [[Katakura Kagetsuna]], became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to [[Date Masamune]]).
In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''[[karō]]'' in the [[Sendai Domain]]. Their personal fief was centered at [[Shiroishi Castle]] (modern-day [[Shiroishi, Miyagi]]).
[[Shigenobu Katakura]], the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family.
==Head Family== # Katakura Kagekatsu # Katakura Kagefusa # Katakura Kagenobu # Katakura Kageharu # Katakura Kagetsune # Katakura Kagetoki # Katakura Kageshige # Katakura Kagesuke # Katakura Kageyuki # Katakura Kagehiro # Katakura Kagemura # Katakura Kageshige # [[Katakura Kagetsuna]] # [[Katakura Shigenaga]] # [[Katakura Kagenaga (2nd)]] # [[Katakura Muranaga]] # [[Katakura Murayasu]] # [[Katakura Muranobu]] # [[Katakura Murasada]] # [[Katakura Murakiyo]] # [[Katakura Muratsune]] # [[Katakura Kagesada]] # [[Katakura Munekage]] # [[Katakura Kuninori]] # [[Katakura Kagenori]] # [[Katakura Kagemitsu]] # [[Katakura Kenkichi]] # [[Katakura Nobumitsu]] # [[Shigenobu Katakura]]
== Others == * [[Katakura Kita]] (1538-1610) was the half-sister of Katakura Kagetsuna and teacher of Date Masamune and Kagetsuna.
==References== *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/katakura.html "Katakura-shi" on Harimaya.com] (9 March 2008) *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www008.upp.so-net.ne.jp/tomeas/katakura.htm Family tree of the Katakura clan] (9 March 2008)
{{Authority control}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Katakura clan}} [[Category:Katakura clan| ]] [[Category:Japanese clans]]
{{Japan-clan-stub}}

{{Use dmy dates|date=March 2020}} {{family name hatnote|Katakura|lang=Japanese}} [[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|140px|The emblem ([[Mon (badge)|''mon'']]) of the Katakura clan]] The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from [[Fujiwara no Toshihito]] by way of [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered [[Mutsu Province]] in the 14th century as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the [[Date clan]], and remained so until 1872. In the [[Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, [[Katakura Kagetsuna]], became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to [[Date Masamune]]).
In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''[[karō]]'' in the [[Sendai Domain]]. Their personal fief was centered at [[Shiroishi Castle]] (modern-day [[Shiroishi, Miyagi]]).
[[Shigenobu Katakura]], the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family.

{{Use dmy dates|date=March 2020}} {{family name hatnote|Katakura|lang=Japanese}} [[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|140px|The emblem ([[Mon (badge)|''mon'']]) of the Katakura clan]] The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from [[Fujiwara no Toshihito]] by way of [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered [[Mutsu Province]] in the 14th century as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the [[Date clan]], and remained so until 1872. In the [[Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, [[Katakura Kagetsuna]], became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to [[Date Masamune]]).
In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''[[karō]]'' in the [[Sendai Domain]]. Their personal fief was centered at [[Shiroishi Castle]] (modern-day [[Shiroishi, Miyagi]]).
[[Shigenobu Katakura]], the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family.
==Head Family== # Katakura Kagekatsu # Katakura Kagefusa # Katakura Kagenobu # Katakura Kageharu # Katakura Kagetsune # Katakura Kagetoki # Katakura Kageshige # Katakura Kagesuke # Katakura Kageyuki # Katakura Kagehiro # Katakura Kagemura # Katakura Kageshige # [[Katakura Kagetsuna]] # [[Katakura Shigenaga]] # [[Katakura Kagenaga (2nd)]] # [[Katakura Muranaga]] # [[Katakura Murayasu]] # [[Katakura Muranobu]] # [[Katakura Murasada]] # [[Katakura Murakiyo]] # [[Katakura Muratsune]] # [[Katakura Kagesada]] # [[Katakura Munekage]] # [[Katakura Kuninori]] # [[Katakura Kagenori]] # [[Katakura Kagemitsu]] # [[Katakura Kenkichi]] # [[Katakura Nobumitsu]] # [[Shigenobu Katakura]]
== Others == * [[Katakura Kita]] (1538-1610) was the half-sister of Katakura Kagetsuna and teacher of Date Masamune and Kagetsuna.
==References== *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/katakura.html "Katakura-shi" on Harimaya.com] (9 March 2008) *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www008.upp.so-net.ne.jp/tomeas/katakura.htm Family tree of the Katakura clan] (9 March 2008)
{{Authority control}}

{{Use dmy dates|date=March 2020}} {{family name hatnote|Katakura|lang=Japanese}} [[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|140px|The emblem ([[Mon (badge)|''mon'']]) of the Katakura clan]] The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from [[Fujiwara no Toshihito]] by way of [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered [[Mutsu Province]] in the 14th century as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the [[Date clan]], and remained so until 1872. In the [[Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, [[Katakura Kagetsuna]], became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to [[Date Masamune]]).
In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''[[karō]]'' in the [[Sendai Domain]]. Their personal fief was centered at [[Shiroishi Castle]] (modern-day [[Shiroishi, Miyagi]]).
[[Shigenobu Katakura]], the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family.
==Head Family== # Katakura Kagekatsu # Katakura Kagefusa # Katakura Kagenobu # Katakura Kageharu # Katakura Kagetsune # Katakura Kagetoki # Katakura Kageshige # Katakura Kagesuke # Katakura Kageyuki # Katakura Kagehiro # Katakura Kagemura # Katakura Kageshige # [[Katakura Kagetsuna]] # [[Katakura Shigenaga]] # [[Katakura Kagenaga (2nd)]] # [[Katakura Muranaga]] # [[Katakura Murayasu]] # [[Katakura Muranobu]] # [[Katakura Murasada]] # [[Katakura Murakiyo]] # [[Katakura Muratsune]] # [[Katakura Kagesada]] # [[Katakura Munekage]] # [[Katakura Kuninori]] # [[Katakura Kagenori]] # [[Katakura Kagemitsu]] # [[Katakura Kenkichi]] # [[Katakura Nobumitsu]] # [[Shigenobu Katakura]]
== Others == * [[Katakura Kita]] (1538-1610) was the half-sister of Katakura Kagetsuna and teacher of Date Masamune and Kagetsuna.
==References== *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/katakura.html "Katakura-shi" on Harimaya.com] (9 March 2008) *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www008.upp.so-net.ne.jp/tomeas/katakura.htm Family tree of the Katakura clan] (9 March 2008)

{{Use dmy dates|date=March 2020}} {{family name hatnote|Katakura|lang=Japanese}} [[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|140px|The emblem ([[Mon (badge)|''mon'']]) of the Katakura clan]] The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from [[Fujiwara no Toshihito]] by way of [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered [[Mutsu Province]] in the 14th century as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the [[Date clan]], and remained so until 1872. In the [[Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, [[Katakura Kagetsuna]], became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to [[Date Masamune]]).
In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''[[karō]]'' in the [[Sendai Domain]]. Their personal fief was centered at [[Shiroishi Castle]] (modern-day [[Shiroishi, Miyagi]]).
[[Shigenobu Katakura]], the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family.
==Head Family== # Katakura Kagekatsu # Katakura Kagefusa # Katakura Kagenobu # Katakura Kageharu # Katakura Kagetsune # Katakura Kagetoki # Katakura Kageshige # Katakura Kagesuke # Katakura Kageyuki # Katakura Kagehiro # Katakura Kagemura # Katakura Kageshige # [[Katakura Kagetsuna]] # [[Katakura Shigenaga]] # [[Katakura Kagenaga (2nd)]] # [[Katakura Muranaga]] # [[Katakura Murayasu]] # [[Katakura Muranobu]] # [[Katakura Murasada]] # [[Katakura Murakiyo]] # [[Katakura Muratsune]] # [[Katakura Kagesada]] # [[Katakura Munekage]] # [[Katakura Kuninori]] # [[Katakura Kagenori]] # [[Katakura Kagemitsu]] # [[Katakura Kenkichi]] # [[Katakura Nobumitsu]] # [[Shigenobu Katakura]]
== Others == * [[Katakura Kita]] (1538-1610) was the half-sister of Katakura Kagetsuna and teacher of Date Masamune and Kagetsuna.
==References== *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/katakura.html "Katakura-shi" on Harimaya.com] (9 March 2008) *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www008.upp.so-net.ne.jp/tomeas/katakura.htm Family tree of the Katakura clan] (9 March 2008)
{{Authority control}}

The family entered [[Mutsu Province]] in the 14th century as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the [[Date clan]], and remained so until 1872. In the [[Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, [[Katakura Kagetsuna]], became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to [[Date Masamune]]).
In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''[[karō]]'' in the [[Sendai Domain]]. Their personal fief was centered at [[Shiroishi Castle]] (modern-day [[Shiroishi, Miyagi]]).
[[Shigenobu Katakura]], the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family.
==Head Family== # Katakura Kagekatsu # Katakura Kagefusa # Katakura Kagenobu # Katakura Kageharu # Katakura Kagetsune # Katakura Kagetoki # Katakura Kageshige # Katakura Kagesuke # Katakura Kageyuki # Katakura Kagehiro # Katakura Kagemura # Katakura Kageshige # [[Katakura Kagetsuna]] # [[Katakura Shigenaga]] # [[Katakura Kagenaga (2nd)]] # [[Katakura Muranaga]] # [[Katakura Murayasu]] # [[Katakura Muranobu]] # [[Katakura Murasada]] # [[Katakura Murakiyo]] # [[Katakura Muratsune]] # [[Katakura Kagesada]] # [[Katakura Munekage]] # [[Katakura Kuninori]] # [[Katakura Kagenori]] # [[Katakura Kagemitsu]] # [[Katakura Kenkichi]] # [[Katakura Nobumitsu]] # [[Shigenobu Katakura]]
== Others == * [[Katakura Kita]] (1538-1610) was the half-sister of Katakura Kagetsuna and teacher of Date Masamune and Kagetsuna.
==References== *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/katakura.html "Katakura-shi" on Harimaya.com] (9 March 2008) *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www008.upp.so-net.ne.jp/tomeas/katakura.htm Family tree of the Katakura clan] (9 March 2008)
{{Authority control}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katakura clan]][[fr:Clan Katakura]][[it:Clan Katakura]][[ja:片倉氏]][[pt:Clã Katakura]][[zh:片倉氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q15149847]] Wikidata name: Katakura clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q15149847]] Wikidata name: Katakura clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2020}} {{family name hatnote|Katakura|lang=Japanese}} [[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|140px|The emblem ([[:en:Mon (badge)|''mon'']]) of the Katakura clan]] The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from [[:en:Fujiwara no Toshihito|Fujiwara no Toshihito]] by way of [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] in the 14th century as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the [[:en:Date clan|Date clan]], and remained so until 1872. In the [[:en:Sengoku era|Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, [[:en:Katakura Kagetsuna|Katakura Kagetsuna]], became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]]).
In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''[[:en:Karō|Karō]]'' in the [[:en:Sendai Domain|Sendai Domain]]. Their personal fief was centered at [[:en:Shiroishi Castle|Shiroishi Castle]] (modern-day [[:en:Shiroishi, Miyagi|Shiroishi, Miyagi]]).
[[:en:Shigenobu Katakura|Shigenobu Katakura]], the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family.
==Head Family== # Katakura Kagekatsu # Katakura Kagefusa # Katakura Kagenobu # Katakura Kageharu # Katakura Kagetsune # Katakura Kagetoki # Katakura Kageshige # Katakura Kagesuke # Katakura Kageyuki # Katakura Kagehiro # Katakura Kagemura # Katakura Kageshige # [[:en:Katakura Kagetsuna|Katakura Kagetsuna]] # [[:en:Katakura Shigenaga|Katakura Shigenaga]] # [[:en:Katakura Kagenaga (2nd)|Katakura Kagenaga (2nd)]] # [[:en:Katakura Muranaga|Katakura Muranaga]] # [[:en:Katakura Murayasu|Katakura Murayasu]] # [[:en:Katakura Muranobu|Katakura Muranobu]] # [[:en:Katakura Murasada|Katakura Murasada]] # [[:en:Katakura Murakiyo|Katakura Murakiyo]] # [[:en:Katakura Muratsune|Katakura Muratsune]] # [[:en:Katakura Kagesada|Katakura Kagesada]] # [[:en:Katakura Munekage|Katakura Munekage]] # [[:en:Katakura Kuninori|Katakura Kuninori]] # [[:en:Katakura Kagenori|Katakura Kagenori]] # [[:en:Katakura Kagemitsu|Katakura Kagemitsu]] # [[:en:Katakura Kenkichi|Katakura Kenkichi]] # [[:en:Katakura Nobumitsu|Katakura Nobumitsu]] # [[:en:Shigenobu Katakura|Shigenobu Katakura]]
== Others == * [[:en:Katakura Kita|Katakura Kita]] (1538-1610) was the half-sister of Katakura Kagetsuna and teacher of Date Masamune and Kagetsuna.
==References== *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/katakura.html "Katakura-shi" on Harimaya.com] (9 March 2008) *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www008.upp.so-net.ne.jp/tomeas/katakura.htm Family tree of the Katakura clan] (9 March 2008)
{{Authority control}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katakura clan]][[fr:Clan Katakura]][[it:Clan Katakura]][[ja:片倉氏]][[pt:Clã Katakura]][[zh:片倉氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katakura clan]][[fr:Clan Katakura]][[it:Clan Katakura]][[ja:片倉氏]][[pt:Clã Katakura]][[zh:片倉氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katakura clan]][[fr:Clan Katakura]][[it:Clan Katakura]][[ja:片倉氏]][[pt:Clã Katakura]][[zh:片倉氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katakura clan]][[fr:Clan Katakura]][[it:Clan Katakura]][[ja:片倉氏]][[pt:Clã Katakura]][[zh:片倉氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q15149847]] Wikidata name: Katakura clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q15149847]] Wikidata name: Katakura clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2020}} {{family name hatnote|Katakura|lang=Japanese}} [[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|140px|The emblem ([[:en:Mon (badge)|''mon'']]) of the Katakura clan]] The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from [[:en:Fujiwara no Toshihito|Fujiwara no Toshihito]] by way of [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] in the 14th century as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the [[:en:Date clan|Date clan]], and remained so until 1872. In the [[:en:Sengoku era|Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, [[:en:Katakura Kagetsuna|Katakura Kagetsuna]], became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]]).
In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''[[:en:Karō|Karō]]'' in the [[:en:Sendai Domain|Sendai Domain]]. Their personal fief was centered at [[:en:Shiroishi Castle|Shiroishi Castle]] (modern-day [[:en:Shiroishi, Miyagi|Shiroishi, Miyagi]]).
[[:en:Shigenobu Katakura|Shigenobu Katakura]], the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family.
==Head Family== # Katakura Kagekatsu # Katakura Kagefusa # Katakura Kagenobu # Katakura Kageharu # Katakura Kagetsune # Katakura Kagetoki # Katakura Kageshige # Katakura Kagesuke # Katakura Kageyuki # Katakura Kagehiro # Katakura Kagemura # Katakura Kageshige # [[:en:Katakura Kagetsuna|Katakura Kagetsuna]] # [[:en:Katakura Shigenaga|Katakura Shigenaga]] # [[:en:Katakura Kagenaga (2nd)|Katakura Kagenaga (2nd)]] # [[:en:Katakura Muranaga|Katakura Muranaga]] # [[:en:Katakura Murayasu|Katakura Murayasu]] # [[:en:Katakura Muranobu|Katakura Muranobu]] # [[:en:Katakura Murasada|Katakura Murasada]] # [[:en:Katakura Murakiyo|Katakura Murakiyo]] # [[:en:Katakura Muratsune|Katakura Muratsune]] # [[:en:Katakura Kagesada|Katakura Kagesada]] # [[:en:Katakura Munekage|Katakura Munekage]] # [[:en:Katakura Kuninori|Katakura Kuninori]] # [[:en:Katakura Kagenori|Katakura Kagenori]] # [[:en:Katakura Kagemitsu|Katakura Kagemitsu]] # [[:en:Katakura Kenkichi|Katakura Kenkichi]] # [[:en:Katakura Nobumitsu|Katakura Nobumitsu]] # [[:en:Shigenobu Katakura|Shigenobu Katakura]]
== Others == * [[:en:Katakura Kita|Katakura Kita]] (1538-1610) was the half-sister of Katakura Kagetsuna and teacher of Date Masamune and Kagetsuna.
==References== *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/katakura.html "Katakura-shi" on Harimaya.com] (9 March 2008) *{{in lang|ja}} [http://www008.upp.so-net.ne.jp/tomeas/katakura.htm Family tree of the Katakura clan] (9 March 2008)
{{Authority control}}

{{family name hatnote|Katakura|lang=Japanese}}
[[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|140px|The emblem ([[:en:Mon (badge)|''mon'']]) of the Katakura clan]]
The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from {{ill|Fujiwara no Toshihito|en|Fujiwara no Toshihito|WD=Q3090673|lt=Fujiwara no Toshihito}} by way of [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}} in the 14th century as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the {{ill|Date clan|en|Date clan|WD=Q1151061|lt=Date clan}}, and remained so until 1872. In the [[:en:Sengoku era|Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, {{ill|Katakura Kagetsuna|en|Katakura Kagetsuna|WD=Q843226|lt=Katakura Kagetsuna}}, became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to {{ill|Date Masamune|en|Date Masamune|WD=Q311183|lt=Date Masamune}}).

In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''{{ill|Karō|en|Karō|WD=Q1316445|lt=Karō}}'' in the {{ill|Sendai Domain|en|Sendai Domain|WD=Q1062182|lt=Sendai Domain}}. Their personal fief was centered at {{ill|Shiroishi Castle|en|Shiroishi Castle|WD=Q1037902|lt=Shiroishi Castle}} (modern-day {{ill|Shiroishi, Miyagi|en|Shiroishi, Miyagi|WD=Q823489|lt=Shiroishi, Miyagi}}).

{{ill|Shigenobu Katakura|en|Shigenobu Katakura|WD=Q2388329|lt=Shigenobu Katakura}}, the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family.

==Head Family==

== Others ==
* {{ill|Katakura Kita|en|Katakura Kita|WD=Q11569520|lt=Katakura Kita}} (1538-1610) was the half-sister of Katakura Kagetsuna and teacher of Date Masamune and Kagetsuna.

{{ill|Shigenobu Katakura|en|Shigenobu Katakura|WD=Q2388329|lt=Shigenobu Katakura}}, the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family.

{{Use dmy dates|date=March 2020}}
{{family name hatnote|Katakura|lang=Japanese}}
[[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|140px|The emblem ([[:en:Mon (badge)|''mon'']]) of the Katakura clan]]
The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from {{ill|Fujiwara no Toshihito|en|Fujiwara no Toshihito|WD=Q3090673|lt=Fujiwara no Toshihito}} by way of [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}} in the 14th century as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the {{ill|Date clan|en|Date clan|WD=Q1151061|lt=Date clan}}, and remained so until 1872. In the [[:en:Sengoku era|Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, {{ill|Katakura Kagetsuna|en|Katakura Kagetsuna|WD=Q843226|lt=Katakura Kagetsuna}}, became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to {{ill|Date Masamune|en|Date Masamune|WD=Q311183|lt=Date Masamune}}).

In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''{{ill|Karō|en|Karō|WD=Q1316445|lt=Karō}}'' in the {{ill|Sendai Domain|en|Sendai Domain|WD=Q1062182|lt=Sendai Domain}}. Their personal fief was centered at {{ill|Shiroishi Castle|en|Shiroishi Castle|WD=Q1037902|lt=Shiroishi Castle}} (modern-day {{ill|Shiroishi, Miyagi|en|Shiroishi, Miyagi|WD=Q823489|lt=Shiroishi, Miyagi}}).

{{ill|Shigenobu Katakura|en|Shigenobu Katakura|WD=Q2388329|lt=Shigenobu Katakura}}, the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family.

==Head Family==

# {{ill|Katakura Kagenaga (2nd)|en|Katakura Kagenaga (2nd)|WD=Q2814490|lt=Katakura Kagenaga (2nd)}}

# {{ill|Katakura Kagemitsu|en|Katakura Kagemitsu|WD=Q6375037|lt=Katakura Kagemitsu}}
# {{ill|Katakura Kenkichi|en|Katakura Kenkichi|WD=Q4352170|lt=Katakura Kenkichi}}
# {{ill|Katakura Nobumitsu|en|Katakura Nobumitsu|WD=Q6375041|lt=Katakura Nobumitsu}}
# {{ill|Shigenobu Katakura|en|Shigenobu Katakura|WD=Q2388329|lt=Shigenobu Katakura}}

== Others ==
* {{ill|Katakura Kita|en|Katakura Kita|WD=Q11569520|lt=Katakura Kita}} (1538-1610) was the half-sister of Katakura Kagetsuna and teacher of Date Masamune and Kagetsuna.

==References==

{{Use dmy dates|date=March 2020}}
{{family name hatnote|Katakura|lang=Japanese}}
[[Image:Kuyo.svg|thumb|right|140px|The emblem ([[:en:Mon (badge)|''mon'']]) of the Katakura clan]]
The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from {{ill|Fujiwara no Toshihito|en|Fujiwara no Toshihito|WD=Q3090673|lt=Fujiwara no Toshihito|1=Fujiwara no Toshihito}} by way of [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}} in the 14th century as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the {{ill|Date clan|en|Date clan|WD=Q1151061|lt=Date clan|1=Date clan}}, and remained so until 1872. In the [[:en:Sengoku era|Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, {{ill|Katakura Kagetsuna|en|Katakura Kagetsuna|WD=Q843226|lt=Katakura Kagetsuna|1=Katakura Kagetsuna}}, became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to {{ill|Date Masamune|en|Date Masamune|WD=Q311183|lt=Date Masamune|1=Date Masamune}}).

In the Edo period, the heads of the Katakura clan were hereditary ''{{ill|Karō|en|Karō|WD=Q1316445|lt=Karō|1=Karō}}'' in the {{ill|Sendai Domain|en|Sendai Domain|WD=Q1062182|lt=Sendai Domain|1=Sendai Domain}}. Their personal fief was centered at {{ill|Shiroishi Castle|en|Shiroishi Castle|WD=Q1037902|lt=Shiroishi Castle|1=Shiroishi Castle}} (modern-day {{ill|Shiroishi, Miyagi|en|Shiroishi, Miyagi|WD=Q823489|lt=Shiroishi, Miyagi|1=Shiroishi, Miyagi}}).

{{ill|Shigenobu Katakura|en|Shigenobu Katakura|WD=Q2388329|lt=Shigenobu Katakura|1=Shigenobu Katakura}}, the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family.

The {{nihongo|'''Katakura clan'''|片倉氏|Katakura-shi}} is a Japanese family which claims its descent from {{ill|Fujiwara no Toshihito|en|Fujiwara no Toshihito|qid=Q3090673|lt=Fujiwara no Toshihito|1=Fujiwara no Toshihito}} by way of [[Katō Kagekado]]. The family entered {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|qid=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}} in the 14th century as subordinates of the [[Ōsaki clan]]. However, in 1532, they became retainers of the {{ill|Date clan|en|Date clan|qid=Q1151061|lt=Date clan|1=Date clan}}, and remained so until 1872. In the [[:en:Sengoku era|Sengoku era]], the Katakura took part in all the major campaigns of the Date clan. The family's head, {{ill|Katakura Kagetsuna|en|Katakura Kagetsuna|qid=Q843226|lt=Katakura Kagetsuna|1=Katakura Kagetsuna}}, became renowned throughout the country, even receiving praise from {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, who granted Kagetsuna a fief (thereby bypassing Kagetsuna's status as vassal to {{ill|Date Masamune|en|Date Masamune|qid=Q311183|lt=Date Masamune|1=Date Masamune}}).

Their personal fief was centered at {{ill|Shiroishi Castle|en|Shiroishi Castle|qid=Q1037902|lt=Shiroishi Castle|1=Shiroishi Castle}} (modern-day {{ill|Shiroishi, Miyagi|en|Shiroishi, Miyagi|qid=Q823489|lt=Shiroishi, Miyagi|1=Shiroishi, Miyagi}}).

{{ill|Shigenobu Katakura|en|Shigenobu Katakura|qid=Q2388329|lt=Shigenobu Katakura|1=Shigenobu Katakura}}, the current chief priest of Sendai's [[Aoba Shrine]], is a direct descendant of this family.
